Summary: Traditional AI perceives/reasons/acts (pattern recognition, decision-making); GenAI generates new content (text, image, code).
AI → Machine Learning → Deep Learning → Generative AI; each layer builds on the previous.
Model splits prompt into tokens, predicts next most likely token, repeats until done.
GenAI predicts tokens, not concepts; your role shifts from creator to QA; hallucinations are a feature of how it works.
Treat every prompt as an instruction set; specificity drives output quality.
Direct, single instruction with no examples or context. Best for general knowledge and quick answers.
Provide context, examples, or prior info to shape responses. Should be ~80% of your prompting.
Ground responses by providing a specific source URL, document, or data. Process: Query → Retrieve → Augment → Generate.
Ask the AI to walk through each logical step in its reasoning before reaching a conclusion.
Generate multiple distinct reasoning paths or options simultaneously for comparison.
Ask AI to identify what you haven't considered, improve your prompt, or stress-test your thinking.
Use hashtag-prefixed labels (#Role, #Task, #Audience, #Format) to help the AI parse intent.
Use brackets to mark placeholders, optional parameters, or variables for repeatable templates.
Controls randomness/creativity. Low (0–0.3) = factual; high (0.7–1.0) = creative.
Limits word-choice pool to most probable set; pair with temperature for coherence.
Penalizes tokens already used regardless of count; increases word variety.
Penalizes tokens by frequency of prior use; lower for structured/compliance content.
Define who the AI should act as, the task, and the output format.
Describe current pain, desired future state, and the bridging solution.
Five-part framework with built-in self-review for detailed deliverables.
Categorize tasks into Core/Important/Surface/Noise/Remove to prioritize.
Use AI as 'intellectual gravity' to expose weak reasoning and blind spots before committing.
Identify recurring task → define essential inputs → sequence and name → test and refine.
A persistent instruction set applied to every interaction in a session; shapes default tone and behavior.
Eliminates repeating context, maintains consistency, and keeps AI focused on your role.

Topic: How to build a system prompt (4 steps)
Summary: Define Role & Voice → Set Boundaries → Clarify Style/Format → Include Persistence Rules.
Stages: Prompting → Structured Prompting → Tool Use → AI Agents (autonomous, multi-step).
Goal orientation, planning loop, tool access, memory/context, iteration & refinement.

Topic: When to use an agent vs a prompt
Summary: Use agents for multi-step, dynamic, tool-using tasks; skip for single transformations or one-off content.
Risks: hallucinated tool calls, looping, security exposure, cost creep. Controls: human-in-the-loop, usage caps, logging, scope.
Assess need → Quantify pain → Prioritize → Align stakeholders → Plan for resilience.
Score 1–5 on each axis; quick wins, strategic projects, fill-ins, avoid.
Four-tier resilience plan with hand-off triggers for each tier.
One-page lookup matching technique → when to use → trigger phrase.
